name: Individual Apple Tarts
recipeIngredient: Cold Butter - 1/2 cup, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
Pastries:
1. Combine flour and salt in a large mixing bowl.
2. Using your fingertips or pastry cutter, rub butter into the flour until it resembles pea-sized pieces.
3. Gradually add ice water (up to 1/3 cup) and gently knead just enough for the ingredients to hold together.
4. If necessary, add more water to help the dough stick together.
5. Wrap in plastic and refrigerate until firm, about 1 hour.
6. Divide into 6 pieces and shape each piece into a round that is approximately 1/8 inch thick on a lightly floured surface.
7. Cut each round into circles about 5 1/2 inches in diameter.
8. Place on parchment-lined cookie sheets; refrigerate at least 30 minutes.
Filling:
1. Spread applesauce (homemade or store-bought) evenly over the pastry, leaving space near the edges.
2. Arrange apple slices overlapping slightly in a circle on top of the applesauce.
3. Brush the apples with butter and sprinkle with sugar.
4. Bake at preheated 350°F oven until the apples are tender and the bottom of the pastry is golden brown, about 1 hour.
5. Heat apricot jam with water over low heat; strain through a sieve into a bowl.
6. Brush the hot jam mixture over the apples.
7. Serve warm with ice cream or crème anglaise. Makes: 6 tarts
